function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

REPLACE MODE (mode='replace', default): find a unique string and replace it. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, path, old_string, new_string.
PATCH MODE (mode='patch'): apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, patch.

mode.description: Edit mode. 'replace' (default): requires path + old_string + new_string. 'patch': requires patch content only.

path.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. File path to edit.

old_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include surrounding context lines to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Replacement text. Pass empty string '' to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
REQUIRED when mode='patch'. V4A format patch content. Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

**Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com  
**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay  
**Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica)

## Core Architecture

- **Framework:** `@ai-whisperers/*` package ecosystem (client imports via npm `file:` links)
- **Pages Router** (Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5)
- **Content:** JSON-driven — `content/es.json`, `nexa-pages/*.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`
- **Loading:** `src/lib/loader.ts` with 60s in-memory TTL cache (replaces raw `readFileSync`)
- **Section map:** 26 components in `SECTION_MAP` (see `docs/02-site/COMPONENT_REGISTRY.md`)
- **Types:** 30+ interfaces in `src/types.ts` define the full data contract
- **Styling:** `src/theme.ts` — 20+ color tokens, all inline styles reference this

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `content/es.json` | All text content (100KB, 15+ page sections) |
| `content/en.json`, `nl.json`, `de.json` | Multi-locale content (currently only ES served) |
| `nexa-pages/*.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82 entries across 14 categories |
| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
| `src/pages/[slug].tsx` | Main page renderer — SSR, SECTION_MAP dispatch |
| `src/pages/index.tsx` | Homepage renderer (separate file) |
| `src/pages/blog/[slug].tsx` | Individual blog post renderer — MDX-driven SSR |
| `src/lib/loader.ts` | Shared JSON loader with 60s cache |
| `src/types.ts` | 30+ TypeScript interfaces |
| `src/theme.ts` | Brand design tokens |
| `scripts/screenshot-all.mjs` | Full-page screenshot automation — builds, starts server, captures 22 pages |
| `docs/06-marketing/testimonials-google-form.md` | Google Form template for collecting client testimonials |
| `content/blog/posts-en.json` | English full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `content/blog/posts-nl.json` | Dutch full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `content/blog/posts-de.json` | German full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `blog/en/*.mdx` | 16 English MDX blog articles (6 originals + 10 translated from ES) |
| `blog/nl/*.mdx` | 11 Dutch MDX blog articles (1 original + 10 translated from ES) |
| `blog/de/*.mdx` | 10 German MDX blog articles (translated from ES) |

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build
npm run screenshots      # Full-page screenshot automation (22 pages → screenshots/<iter>/)
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

Requires `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` for `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` (if building with unpublished packages).

## Documentation (docs/)

| Category | Contents |
|----------|----------|
| `00-architecture/` | Core framework, bridge points, data flow, standards |
| `01-client/` | Stakeholder info, intake, questionnaire responses |
| `02-site/` | Architecture, audit, improvement plan, DNS, component registry |
| `03-brand/` | Brand guide, tokens, image prompts, social assets |
| `04-images/` | Image manifest (111 images), prompt library |
| `05-content/` | Locales, blog catalog, editorial calendar |
| `06-marketing/` | Comparisons, email nurture, FAQ, lead magnets, ads, testimonials |
| `07-seo/` | Keyword strategy, content gaps |
| `08-integrations/` | HubSpot, Mailchimp, GA4, WhatsApp AI bridge |
| `09-market-intelligence/` | Solstein analysis, market sizing, competition, financial model |
| `10-deployment/` | Deployment runbook, CI/CD |
| `11-launch/` | Launch runbook, pre-launch checklist |
| `12-factory/` | NEW_CLIENT_BOOTSTRAP.md — template for creating new client repos |

## Critical Notes

- `is_demo: true` in site.json — flip when placeholders replaced
- Team portraits, testimonials, and stock imagery are AI placeholders as of May 2026
- Nexa's primary domain (nexaparaguay.com) still points to Shopify — needs DNS A record to 72.61.44.159
- GA4 measurement ID: G-XE49GLEP34
- See `docs/00-architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` for the Core-to-Client bridge explanation

## WhatsApp AI Bot Setup

|------|-------|
| Bridge URL | https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud |
| Nexa client instance | `client-nexa-paraguay-5138` (phone: [REDACTED_PHONE]) |
| Mode | `ventas` (sales) — auto-qualifies leads |
| Personality | Loaded with full FAQ, programs, tax data, qualification flow |
| Evolution instance | `nexa-paraguay` |
| QR scan image | `public/qr-nexa-whatsapp.png` |
| **NEXT STEP** | Team must scan QR from WhatsApp (Settings → Linked Devices) |

After QR scan: AI responds in EN/NL/DE/ES, qualifies leads (hot/warm/cold), books consultations. See `docs/08-integrations/whatsapp-ai-bridge.md`.

{"content": "     1|const fs = require('fs')
     2|const path = require('path')
     3|
     4|interface CacheEntry { data: any; timestamp: number }
     5|const cache = new Map<string, CacheEntry>()
     6|const TTL = 60_000
     7|
     8|export function loadJSON(dir: string, file: string): any {
     9|  const fullPath = path.join(dir, file)
    10|  const now = Date.now()
    11|  const cached = cache.get(fullPath)
    12|  if (cached && (now - cached.timestamp) < TTL) return cached.data
    13|  try {
    14|    const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(fullPath, 'utf-8'))
    15|    cache.set(fullPath, { data, timestamp: now })
    16|    return data
    17|  } catch { return null }
    18|}
    19|
    20|export function loadContentJSON(dir: string, file: string): any {
    21|  const fullPath = path.join(dir, file)
    22|  const cached = cache.get(fullPath)
    23|  if (cached && (Date.now() - cached.timestamp) < TTL) return cached.data
    24|  const data = loadJSON(dir, file)
    25|  if (data) cache.set(fullPath, { data, timestamp: Date.now() })
    26|  return data
    27|}

{"content": "     1|import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
     2|
     3|const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de']
     4|const DEFAULT = 'nl'
     5|const LOCALE_COOKIE = 'NEXT_LOCALE'
     6|
     7|function getLocale(request: NextRequest): string {
     8|  const pathname = request.nextUrl.pathname
     9|  const segments = pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
    10|  if (segments.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(segments[0])) return segments[0]
    11|  const cookie = request.cookies.get(LOCALE_COOKIE)?.value
    12|  if (cookie && LOCALES.includes(cookie)) return cookie
    13|  return DEFAULT
    14|}
    15|
    16|export function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
    17|  const pathname = request.nextUrl.pathname
    18|  if (pathname.startsWith('/_next') || pathname.startsWith('/api') || pathname.startsWith('/images') || pathname.startsWith('/fonts') || pathname === '/favicon.ico' || pathname.includes('.')) return NextResponse.next()
    19|  const segments = pathname.split('/').filter(Boolean)
    20|  if (segments.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(segments[0])) return NextResponse.next()
    21|  const locale = getLocale(request)
    22|  const url = new URL(`/${locale}${pathname === '/' ? '' : pathname}`, request.url)
    23|  const res = NextResponse.redirect(url)
    24|  res.cookies.set(LOCALE_COOKIE, locale, { maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 })
    25|  return res
    26|}

### 2. DOCKER SETUP

**Dockerfile** (multi-stage):
- **Deps stage**: `node:20-alpine`, installs `libc6-compat`, runs `npm install --legacy-peer-deps`
- **Builder stage**: Copies deps, runs `npm run build`
- **Runner stage**: `node:20-alpine`, standalone output, copies `.next/standalone`, `.next/static`, `public/`, `images.json`, `content/`, `nexa-pages/`
- **Key**: Content-driven deployment — JSON files are baked into the image

**docker-compose.yml**:
- Single service `web`, build from `.`
- **Traefik labels**: HTTPS via Let's Encrypt, `websecure` entrypoint, compress middleware
- Network: External `agent-net`

**`.dockerignore`**: `node_modules`, `.next`, `.git`

**What should be abstracted**: The Dockerfile multi-stage pattern and docker-compose Traefik labels are **highly reusable**. The base should provide:
- A standard Dockerfile template (`Dockerfile.base` or `npm run scaffold:docker`)
- Docker compose template with Traefik labels (just change `Host()` rules and service name)

**Client-specific**: Image name (`nexa-paraguay:prod`), hostnames, service name, content JSON paths

### 3. CONTENT ARCHITECTURE

**Content storage model**: JSON-driven, no CMS—files in repo

A. **`content/{locale}.json`** (e.g., `es.json` = 213KB):
   - `siteName`, `tagline`, `placeholders` (country tokens like `{{taxRate}}`)
   - `navigation` → `Header` consumes
   - `{pageName}` → keyed by page (e.g., `home`, `programasPage`, `servicesPage`)
   - Each page section has: `hero`, `faq`, `trust`, `process`, `programs`, `testimonials`, `cta`, etc.
   - Deeply nested: `home.hero.headline`, `home.stats.items[].value`
   - `footer` → `Footer` consumes

B. **`nexa-pages/{slug}.json`**: Page config defining section ordering
   ```json
   {
     "id": "programas",
     "targetKeyword": "programas residencia Paraguay",
     "slug": "programas",
     "titleKey": "programasPage.seo.title",
     "descriptionKey": "programasPage.seo.description",
     "sections": [
       {"id": "hero", "variant": "standard", "content": "programasPage.hero"},
       {"id": "faq", "content": "programasPage.faq"},
       {"id": "cta-banner", "content": "programasPage.cta"}
     ]
   }
   ```
   - `id` maps to SECTION_MAP key
   - `content` is a dot-path into the locale JSON (e.g., `programasPage.hero`)
   - `variant` optional styling variant
   - `enabledWhen` optional conditional content check

C. **`images.json`**: 111-image manifest
   ```json
   {
     "tenant": "nexa-paraguay",
     "basePath": "/images",
     "fallback": "/images/brand/placeholder.png",
     "images": {
       "brand": { "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "alt": "...", "fallbackSrc": "...", "srcSvg": "..." } },
       "hero": { "localizedEs": { "src": "...", "mobileSrc": "..." } }
     }
   }
   ```
   - Reference via `@img:hero.localizedEs` syntax → `resolveImage()` translates

D. **`site.json`**: Global config
   - `vertical`, `country`, `domain`, `locales`, `features`, `contact`, `integrations`, `chrome` (layout config), `bookingUrl`, `social`

E. **`testimonials.json`**: Separated from content, injected at load time in `loadPageData()`

F. **Locales**: `nl` (default), `en`, `de`, `es` — ES is primary content; others are translations

**What should be abstracted**:
- The **entire content architecture** (JSON file schema, dot-path resolution, page config format) should be standardized in base as `@ai-whisperers/content` or enhanced in `@ai-whisperers/client-kit`
- `loadJSON()` with TTL cache → already partially exists in `@ai-whisperers/client-kit/src/loader.ts` (but uses async/Supabase; Nexa uses sync `readFileSync`)
- `resolveImage()` → should be in base
- `resolveContent()` dot-path resolver → should be in base
- Page config JSON schema (`nexa-pages/*.json`) → should be in base
- The `titleKey`/`descriptionKey`/`seo` content reference pattern → should be in base

**Client-specific**: Actual text content, image paths, testimonials, page slugs

### 4. COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE

A. **SECTION_MAP Pattern** (in `SectionsRenderer.tsx`):
   ```tsx
   const SECTIONS: Record<string, any> = {
     hero: HeroSection,
     'trust-signals': TrustSection,
     'why-destination': WhyCountrySection,
     services: ServicesSection,
     'process-timeline': ProcessSection,
     'cta-banner': CtaBanner,
     requirements: RequirementsSection,
     'booking-embed': BookingEmbedSection,
     contact: ContactDetailsSection,
   }
   ```
   - **Key insight**: `SectionsRenderer.tsx` (70 lines) is the **most reusable file in the entire repo** — it's a pure pattern with zero client-specific content
   - Each section receives `(pageContent, data, images, locale)` via `SectionComponentProps`
   - `GenericSection` fallback renders any unknown section IDs from content data automatically
   - Unknown IDs fall through to `GenericSection` which auto-renders from JSON data

B. **Section Components** (29 total across `sections.tsx` + `sections-extra.tsx`):
   - `HeroSection` — dark gradient background, headline, CTA buttons, trust badges
   - `StatsSection` — value/label stats grid
   - `TrustSection` — card grid with images
   - `ProgramsSection` — tier cards with highlighted/pricing
   - `ServicesSection` — grouped service cards
   - `WhyCountrySection` — dark-themed pillar cards
   - `FeaturesSection` — feature cards
   - `ProcessSection` — timeline steps with numbers
   - `TestimonialsSection` — review cards
   - `RequirementsSection` — complex document/checklist layout
   - `CtaBanner` — gradient CTA strip
   - `FaqSection` — accordion (basic)
   - `FaqSearchSection` — accordion with search filter (more complex)
   - `BlogSection` — post cards grid
   - `TeamSection` — member cards
   - `PrivacyAccordion` — dark/light accordion variant
   - `GlossarySection` — term/definition cards
   - `NewsletterSection` — email signup with input
   - `StorySection` — paragraph text
   - `PillarsSection` — dark-themed pillar grid
   - `PageHeroSection` — simple page header
   - `HighlightSection` — stats/features highlight
   - `ComparisonSection` — HTML table
   - `GuidesSection` — downloadable guides
   - `BookingEmbedSection` — WhatsApp booking CTA
   - `CaseStudySection` — interactive case studies with tabs
   - `ContactDetailsSection` — WhatsApp/email/address cards
   - `GallerySection` — image grid
   - `ServiceDetailSection` — detailed service groups
   - `PressReleasesListSection` — press articles
   - `IntakeWizardSection` — multi-step quiz wizard

C. **Theme System** (`src/theme.ts`):
   - 20+ color tokens, radii, shadows, fonts, spacing, sizes, breakpoints, transitions
   - **Important**: Inline styles reference `theme.colors.primary` etc. BUT the CSS/Tailwind uses `@theme` in `globals.css` with the same values hardcoded
   - **Problem**: The `theme.ts` file is NOT imported by components (they use Tailwind classes like `bg-accent`, `text-primary`). The theme.ts seems to be used only occasionally via inline styles.

D. **Layout Components**:
   - `Header` — sticky nav with locale switcher dropdown
   - `Footer` — multi-column footer with copyright
   - `CookieBanner` — GDPR consent banner (localStorage-based)
   - `ExitPopup` + `ExitPopupWrapper` — exit intent lead capture
   - `GatewayPopup` — session reset notification
   - `ErrorBoundary` — section-level error boundary (silent fail)

E. **`content.ts`** — Two key utilities:
   - `resolveContent(content, key)` — dot-path resolver (e.g., `home.hero` → content.home.hero)
   - `resolveImage(images, ref)` — `@img:brand.logo` → `/images/brand/logo.webp`

**What should be abstracted**:
- **`SectionsRenderer.tsx`** — 100% reusable, should be in base
- **`SectionComponentProps` type** — should be in base
- **All section components** (29 components) — **these are the PRIMARY abstraction target**. They contain zero client-specific business logic. The layouts, styling, data shapes are all driven by JSON content.
- **`content.ts`** (resolveContent + resolveImage) — should be in base
- **`ErrorBoundary.tsx`** — already exists in `@ai-whisperers/ui-extras` as a different version
- **Theme tokens structure** (`tokens.json`, `theme.ts`, `globals.css` `@theme` variables) — should be a standard pattern in base

**Client-specific**: The actual nav items, footer links, locale codes vary per client

### 5. SEO PATTERNS

A. **Sitemap** (`src/app/sitemap.ts`): Dynamic, iterates locales × page slugs from disk
   - Generates `alternates` (hreflang) for ES/EN/NL/DE
   - Base URL hardcoded per client
   
B. **Robots** (`public/robots.txt`): Static, has placeholder `SITE_URL_PLACEHOLDER`
   - **Bug**: Not dynamically generated; has a placeholder string
   
C. **Schema Markup** (`public/schema/`):
   - `organization.json` — Organization schema
   - `faq.json` — FAQPage schema with 12 Q&A items
   - Static files served from `/public/schema/`
   - **Note**: These are NOT dynamically generated from content — they're hand-written

D. **Metadata Generation**:
   - `generateMetadata()` in `[slug]/page.tsx`: Reads `pageConfig.title` from nexa-pages JSON
   - Root layout: hardcoded `title: 'Nexa Paraguay'`, `metadataBase: new URL('https://nexaparaguay.com')`
   - **Missing**: OpenGraph images, proper locale alternates in root layout

E. **Security Headers** (`next.config.js`): CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.

**What should be abstracted**:
- **Sitemap generator** — should be a utility in base that reads page configs + locales + base URL
- **Dynamic robots.txt** — should be served via `app/robots.ts` from `site.json`
- **Schema generation utility** — should generate `Organization`, `FAQPage`, `LocalBusiness` JSON-LD from content data dynamically
- **Security headers template** — can be a base config export
- **Metadata helper** — standard metadata generation from page config + content
- **Locale alternates logic** — standard hreflang generation

**Client-specific**: Base URLs, phone numbers, schema content (Q&A items, org details)

### 6. KEY UTILITY FILES

A. **`src/lib/loader.ts`** — Simple JSON file loader with 60s TTL in-memory cache
   ```typescript
   // Already partially abstracted in @ai-whisperers/client-kit/src/loader.ts
   // Nexa's version: sync readFileSync + Map cache
   // client-kit version: async Supabase/URL loader + module state cache
   // **Need**: A unified version supporting both sync (file) and async (Supabase/URL) modes
   ```
   
B. **`src/lib/page-data.ts`** — Content orchestration
   - `loadPageData(locale, slug)` → loads content JSON + page config JSON + images JSON + testimonials JSON
   - **Should be abstracted**: The orchestration pattern (content + pageConfig + images + extras)
   
C. **`src/middleware.ts`** — Locale routing
   - Detects locale from URL path, cookie, or default
   - Redirects `/slug` → `/{locale}/slug`
   - Matcher excludes `/_next`, `/api`, `/images`, `/fonts`, files with extensions
   - **Should be abstracted**: Locale detection and redirection (configurable locales list and default)

D. **`src/theme.ts`** — Design tokens
   - Colors, radii, shadows, fonts, spacing, sizes, breakpoints, transitions
   - **Should be abstracted**: The token structure/interface, with client-specific values injected

E. **`src/types.ts`** — 30+ interfaces
   - `SiteContent`, `PageSectionContent`, `SEOContent`, `Navigation`, `Footer`, `HeroContent`
   - `ProgramTier`, `ProcessStep`, `TestimonialItem`, `FaqItem`, `TeamMember`, etc.
   - **Should be abstracted**: The full type system — it defines the data contract between content JSON and components

F. **`tokens.json`** — Brand palette overrides
   - `palettes.default.colors` — 9 color keys with hex values
   - **Should be abstracted**: The palette schema (already partially in `@ai-whisperers/theme`)

G. **`site.json`** — Global site config
   - `vertical`, `country`, `domain`, `locales`, `features`, `contact`, `integrations`, `chrome`
   - **Should be abstracted**: The schema and how components read from it

H. **`next.config.js`** — Output: standalone, security headers, image caching
   - **Should be abstracted**: Security headers array as a base-preset

### 7. WHATSAPP / CTA PATTERNS

A. **WhatsApp Float Button**: Direct `https://wa.me/{phone}` link
   - Phone: `[REDACTED_PHONE]` (from `site.json` / `content.contact.whatsapp`)
   - **Base already has**: `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` with `WhatsAppFloat` component
   - **Nexa uses**: Inline in `Header.tsx` CTA button and `BookingEmbedSection` — no dedicated float component currently in Nexa's source

B. **Calendly Integration**: None — all booking goes through WhatsApp
   - `bookingUrl` in `site.json`: `https://wa.me/[REDACTED_PHONE]?text=Quiero%20agendar%20una%20consulta%20gratuita`

C. **Booking Form** (`BookingFormSection.tsx`):
   - 3-step wizard: Program selection → Contact info → Confirmation
   - POSTs to `/api/contact` (simple console.log endpoint)
   - **Very client-specific**: Program list hardcoded (Base/Business/Investor/Land)
   - **Should be abstracted**: The multi-step form pattern and API endpoint, with content-driven steps

D. **WhatsApp AI Bot**: External service (not in repo)
   - Bridge at `https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud`
   - QR scan image in `public/qr-nexa-whatsapp.png`

E. **Exit Popup** (`ExitPopup.tsx`):
   - Exit intent + 30s timeout
   - Captures email via `/api/contact`
   - **Base already has**: `@ai-whisperers/ui-extras` with `ExitIntent` component (but simpler — no form/email capture)
   - **Nexa's is more sophisticated**: Has email capture, confirmation state, localStorage dismissal tracking

F. **API Routes**:
   - `POST /api/contact` — Rate-limited (10/hr per IP), logs to console, returns `{ok: true}`
   - `POST /api/delete-data` — GDPR deletion request handler
   - **Should be abstracted**: The rate-limited contact API pattern

**What should be abstracted**:
- WhatsApp float button → already in base (but base's version has hardcoded message; Nexa's would need to make it configurable)
- Contact form API route with rate limiting → reusable pattern
- Booking form **pattern** (not the specific client programs list)
- Exit intent with email capture → enhancement of base's `ExitIntent`
- Integration config schema (Supabase, GA4, HubSpot, Mailchimp) from `site.json`

## Summary: What to Abstract vs What Stays Client-Specific

### HIGH PRIORITY — Should Move to `@ai-whisperers/base`

| Component/File | Suggested Package | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `SectionsRenderer.tsx` | New: `@ai-whisperers/sections` | Pure dispatch logic, zero client-specific |
| All 29 section components | `@ai-whisperers/sections` | CSS/class based, data-driven, all generic |
| `SectionComponentProps` type | `@ai-whisperers/sections` | Standardized interface |
| `content.ts` (resolveContent + resolveImage) | `@ai-whisperers/sections` or `client-kit` | Dot-path + image ref resolution |
| `src/types.ts` (30+ interfaces) | New: `@ai-whisperers/types` or in `client-kit` | Data contract for content JSON schema |
| `loader.ts` (TTL cache) | `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` | Already partial — needs sync mode |
| `page-data.ts` pattern | `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` | Content orchestration loader |
| `middleware.ts` locale routing | New: `@ai-whisperers/i18n` or `client-kit` | Parameters: locales list, default locale |
| `sitemap.ts` generator | New: `@ai-whisperers/seo` | Dynamic sitemap from page configs + locales |
| Security headers in `next.config.js` | `@ai-whisperers/seo` or new `config` pkg | Standard CSP/HSTS/headers preset |
| `Theme` token structure | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | Already partial — needs full shape |
| `tokens.json` schema | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | Palette override schema |
| ExitPopup email capture pattern | `@ai-whisperers/ui-extras` | Enhance existing ExitIntent |
| Rate-limited contact API | New: `@ai-whisperers/api-helpers` | Generic POST handler with rate limiting |

### MEDIUM PRIORITY — Pattern to Standardize

| Pattern | Benefit |
|---|---|
| Dockerfile multi-stage build template | Every client needs this; just change service name |
| docker-compose Traefik labels pattern | Every client needs HTTPS + certbot |
| `site.json` schema (vertical, contact, features, integrations) | Config-driven feature toggles |
| Page config JSON structure (`nexa-pages/*.json`) | Section ordering + content key references |
| Image manifest structure (`images.json`) | Centralized image management |
| Nexa-pages file-based routing generator | Auto-discover pages from filesystem |
| `@theme` CSS variable setup (`globals.css`) | Standard Tailwind v4 theming |
| `.env.example` structure | Standard env vars for all clients |

### CLIENT-SPECIFIC (Stays in Nexa repo)

| Item | Reason |
|---|---|
| `content/{locale}.json` | The actual Spanish/Dutch/English/German content |
| `images.json` values | Actual image paths for this client |
| `public/images/` | All static images (300+ files) |
| `nexa-pages/*.json` content | Section slugs and content key references |
| `site.json` values | Domain, phone, social links, integrations |
| `blog/*.mdx` | Blog post content |
| `testimonials.json` | Testimonial data |
| `site.json` features flags | Feature toggle values |
| `scripts/` (screenshots, SEO monitoring, etc.) | Automation scripts — though some patterns could be abstracted |
| `docs/` | Entirely client-specific documentation |
| Brand colors in `theme.ts` / `tokens.json` | Values are Nexa-specific |
